title: THE WHITE STAG OF DRONE TOWN
description:
The White Stag stands at the edge of a fractured woodland road, dressed for a ceremony whose meaning has been lost. Behind him, a damaged red car carries the debris of another life, while a formation of drones moves silently through the trees.

The painting presents Drone Town as a place where nature, technology and human identity have begun to overwrite one another. The stag, traditionally a symbol of sovereignty and the wild, appears here as a posthuman resident: part aristocrat, part animal, part corrupted image. His white suit suggests authority, but its surface is already being consumed by paint, data and decay.

The road seems to divide and repeat, as though the landscape itself is struggling to render correctly. Nothing is entirely stable. The forest has become an interface, the drones have become its new wildlife, and the figure waits inside the malfunctioning image as both ruler and relic.

title: THE HOPE SEEKER
description:
The Hope Seeker moves through Drone Town beneath a sky of hovering machines and fractured palms. The street is flooded with pink light, but the brightness does not necessarily mean safety. It feels more like a trace, a flare, or something glimpsed at a distance.

She appears part woman, part dancer, part damaged transmission, carrying herself forward through a landscape distorted by surveillance, memory and technological interference. Her body is interrupted by collage and glitch, yet she continues.

The Hope Seeker is not about hope as certainty. It is about the act of looking for it when the world has become unstable. In Drone Town, hope is not waiting at the end of the road. It flickers through the malfunction, briefly visible, just enough to keep moving.

title: À La Recherche de L'espoir (In Search of Hope)
description:
Figures walk, dance and pixelate along the entire length of a dusk suburban strip in Drone Town — motels burning warm at the windows, palms cut black against a bruised teal sky — and cannot hold its own shape. The figures keep transforming: into iridescent collage shards, into a red machine-warped head crowned with antlers that are also rotor-blades, into pixel-sorted ribbons where the trees should be. Overhead, the drones keep watch.

Made through GAN synthesis and meta-collage — machine-generated imagery layered and torn against itself — À La Recherche de L'espoir is a work from Drone Town, where the American suburban dream is run through the machine until it glitches into something tender and strange. The title echoes Proust but inverts his subject: not lost time, but hope, searched for across a landscape already dissolving. What's left flickering on the road is the looking itself.

title: DRONE ANT MAN
description:
Drone Ant Man owned this part of Drone Town. With his glitching suit of teal promises and inky drone helmet, he ran a abandoned car showroom with vehicles that could zip you all the way back to 1985 and still be back in time for dinner at the Drone Diner. 

Swamp Rats was his band and he pasted their bill posters all over this part of Drone Town; more a statement than advertising, he was past selling the dream. 

The cars still ran, technically. You'd turn the key, the dash would flicker through a decade it had never lived in, and the whole thing would fold you back to 1985 — windows down, FM static, the suburb still believing in itself. Drone Ant Man let you take one out on one condition: you came back. Most people came back. The ones who didn't, he never chased. He'd seen the figures.

The Drone Diner kept his table by the window. Black coffee, no order taken — they knew him. He'd sit under the glitch of his own helmet and watch the tail-lights pull out toward the edge of town, where the road stopped resolving and went to blocks.

title: RED TWIN GLITCH
description:
Couture never made it out to Drone Town but glitch did. Press a few buttons and your whole soul could change colour and be glitched beyond this dimension. The Red Twins always wore ink face masks to hide their true identity. It also made them look a bit more menacing which was always needed in Drone Town. You wear your heart on your self in this place and they will chew you up!

The couture houses never sent a single dress past the ring road. Why would they. But glitch arrived on its own, the way weather does, and the Red Twins had learned to wear it well. 

Nobody in Drone Town had ever seen their faces. The ink masks went on before the boots did, black and wet-looking, half-painted and half-grown, and they did two jobs at once. They kept the Twins' real identity somewhere safe. And they made the Twins look like something you didn't want to owe money to. Both were necessary. This was Drone Town.

The rule of the place was the first thing you learned and usually the hard way: wear your heart on the outside, and the town chews you up and pixelates what's left into the gutter. The Twins wore nothing on the outside. That was the entire point of the mask. They'd parked their black car at the red light and let the rest of the street decide whether to look.

title: MANGA STREET SHRINE
collection: THE MANGA MACHINE - GRAFFITI COLLAGE ORIGINALS
description:
This is the Blue Manga Street that Blue Cherub, the leader of the Porcelain Androids loved the most. Tucked away off the Grassmarket-Roppongi Way, it bustled with acid pink madness and graffiti red hearts. 

The Porcelain Android Gang had always based themselves around these parts. Their hybrid souls blended in easily as they posed danced and drank in the techno lifestyle that only they knew. 

Word on the Blue Manga Street was that there was a secret Shrine somewhere in-between the graffiti mayhem and it was linked somehow to the Manga Machine. 

She knew they would have to find it.

Original Graffiti Collage by Miss AL Simpson (1/1 NFT on Ethereum with mixed media painting on board, signed - 51 cm x 76 cm)

Part of the Manga Machine Series by Miss AL Simpson - view the artwork in the virtual exhibition here - https://porcelainandroid.com/graffiti

collection: THE MANGA MACHINE - GRAFFITI COLLAGE ORIGINALS

title: ROUGE RED PORCELAIN
collection: THE MANGA MACHINE - GRAFFITI COLLAGE ORIGINALS
description:
The Grassmarket-Roppongi Way was the longest and best-loved of all the Crypto Tunnels — a supersonic seam stitched from a medieval Scottish market square, its cobbles still keeping the memory of old ghosts, straight into the most exquisitely ruined nightlife district left standing on the planet. You stepped into a close off the Grassmarket and you stepped out into Roppongi rain. Eleven seconds. No passport. No permission asked of anyone living or otherwise.

Rouge Red Porcelain was the leader of the Roppongi Porcelain Android Gang. Red leather coat, beauty for miles and the longest legs this side of Tokyo, she ruled with a red lipstick roar. She liked to patrol The Grassmarket-Roppongi Way with red stiletto boots and a cracked porcelain graffiti dog at her side.


Original Graffiti Collage by Miss AL Simpson (1/1 NFT on Ethereum with mixed media painting on board, signed - 51 cm x 76 cm)

Part of the Manga Machine Series by Miss AL Simpson - view the artwork in the virtual exhibition here - https://porcelainandroid.com/graffiti

title: RETURNING TO HAIFA
description:
"Returning to Haifa" draws on Ghassan Kanafani’s novella of the same name, a work about displacement, memory and the impossible return to a home transformed by history. The piece places Kanafani within a fractured cityscape, surrounded by documents, crowds, broken architecture and painted interruptions. Haifa appears not simply as a destination, but as a place charged with absence, memory and unresolved return.

The words “Returning to Haifa,” “Right of Return,” and “Between the sword and the neck” move through the image as sharp fragments of pressure. They connect the personal with the political: a family, a house, a city, and a history that cannot be neatly recovered. The small figures walking into the composition suggest return as movement, but not resolution.

Kanafani’s portrait sits embedded in the landscape, as if the author is both witness and architect of the story. The red, blue and yellow marks cut across the surface like signals, routes and interruptions. The work holds the tension at the heart of Kanafani’s writing: return is not nostalgia. It is confrontation. It asks what has been lost, what remains, and what it means to come back to a place that has continued without you.

title: THE OLIVE TREE REMEMBERS
description:
"The Olive Tree Remembers" centres on Mahmoud Darwish’s language of land, exile and memory. The olive tree becomes a living archive, holding the hands that planted it, the people displaced from it, and the grief carried through generations.

The work brings together portraits of Darwish across time: the young poet in the document on the left, the larger central face emerging through the landscape, and the quieter standing figure on the right. These versions of him feel suspended between record, witness and memory.

Written fragments move through the surface: “If the olive trees knew the hands that planted them, their oil would become tears” “Write down! I am an Arab,” “Right of Return,” and “Silence for Gaza.” Around them, documents, ruins, olive branches and painted interruptions create a fractured archive of loss and endurance.

The work is not only about mourning. It is about what remains rooted. Memory here grows through the surface, like the olive tree itself.

title: Palestine, Before
description: Digital collage and painting, 2026

title: Palestine, Before (Animated)
description: Animated Digital collage and painting, 2026

title: MADAME EQUALITY
description:
She was promised long before she arrived.

Her name appears in constitutions, speeches, and declarations. Written carefully into the language of the law as if the act of naming her would make her real.

Equality.

But promises have a strange habit of floating above the surface of the world.

Here she stands — composed, luminous, almost ceremonial — a figure dressed in the symbols of justice and reform. Yet the line across her gaze feels less like balance and more like a boundary.

Because equality has always existed first as an idea.

A word repeated often enough that societies begin to believe it has already been achieved.

But ideas do not enforce themselves.

And so Madame Equality waits, suspended somewhere between principle and reality.

Still named.

Still promised.

Still unfinished.

title: NO COUNTRY
description:
On 6 March 2026, UN Women released a global report confirming an uncomfortable truth: no country in the world has achieved full legal equality for women and girls.

For more than a century the world has been telling a different story. Laws were written. Constitutions amended. Equality declared.

But the fragments remain visible.

Broken statues of justice.
Half-finished sentences.
Promises splintering across the surface of the law.

The red cuts through the image like a warning signal — a reminder that what looks stable from a distance often fractures when you step closer.

Because the world likes to believe the question of women’s equality has already been settled.

Yet the evidence says otherwise.

Not one country.

Not yet.

title: SINE LEGE
description:
The pillars are still standing, but the law has slipped out between the cracks.

Once there were promises carved into marble. Rights written down, signed off, stamped and sealed. Women were told the law would hold them steady — scales balanced, justice blind and faithful.

But somewhere along the way the machinery faltered. The rules remain, but the gravity is gone. Paper protections drifting through the air like ash.

The scales still hang.

One side reads without.
The other reads law.

And between them stands a woman — painted in fragments, half myth, half witness — carrying the weight of promises that were never meant to hold.

Sine lege.

Without law.

title: LEX IMPERFECTA
description:
On 6 March 2026, UN Women released a global report confirming a stark reality: no country in the world has achieved full legal equality for women and girls.

For more than a century, governments have promised equality. Laws were written. Constitutions amended. Treaties signed. Women were told the law would protect them.

Yet the protections remain incomplete. Many laws intended to safeguard women are weakly enforced, or structured in ways that make them difficult to enforce in practice.

Lex Imperfecta takes its title from the Roman legal concept describing a law that declares a principle but lacks the force to make it real.

Across the world, women's rights exist on paper. But without enforcement, the promise of equality remains unfinished.

title: STREETWISE
description:
Pale pink Streetwise
gloss lips tell no lies. 

Graffiti hearts sold
on the spray-paint promise.

AI PAINTING AND COLLAGE

title: LOOKING FOR LIKES
description:
Always looking for likes,
Swimming the the sea
of notifications.
DM Addiction was her 
only folly. 


AI PAINTING AND COLLAGE

title: HER FRAGILE HEART
description:
All the signs were there,
Her Fragile Heart
Held close.

She wasn't supposed to have one.
But a Fragile one was better than none.

AI PAINTING AND COLLAGE

title: STREET WISE
description: AI PAINTING AND COLLAGE, MISS AL SIMPSON, 2026.

title: FLAGGED
description:
Palantir sits in her iris like a second pupil.  
ICE stands on her cheek as if the skin were territory.  
A drone hovers, red lights blinking, patient as a cursor.

Grids crawl across her face.  
Borders press into bone.

Across her lips: LIVE FEED.  
Not freedom. Transmission.

She is luminous.  
She is flagged.  
She is still looking back.

title: LIBRA SINE PONDERE
description:
Women walk through ruins carrying scales that hold nothing.

Stone is split. Concrete hangs open like torn paper. Smoke moves without urgency. The sky is too wide.

The gold chains remain immaculate. The bowls hang clean, untouched.

Empty.

They step over rubble. Through collapsed arches. Past broken columns that once held laws upright. No one stops them. No one gathers. No verdict is spoken.

International law survives as symbol. As ritual. As polished metal in a shattered landscape.

Ink runs down their skin like dissolved treaties. A pulse of red flickers at the chest and fades into ash.

The architecture has fallen.

The scales have not.

But nothing weighs upon them.

And still, they carry them forward.

title: BLUE LIBRA
description:
Blue Libra does not carry justice. She remembers it.

In this work, the scales have slipped from her hands and dissolved into atmosphere. What remains is a woman suspended between law and longing, between order and erosion. A strip of fractured gold hovers above her like a relic from a fallen temple, no longer balancing anything, merely witnessing.

Her face emerges through veils of cobalt, ash, and torn typography. Language collapses around her. Letters scatter like broken verdicts. Ink runs vertically, as if gravity itself is uncertain. The gesture is deliberate: when scales hold no weight, truth liquefies.

Blue is not decorative here. It is juridical. It is bruised air. It is the colour of distance between principle and power.

She stands in a dark, translucent garment that feels half-armour, half-memory. Her body remains intact, poised, composed. Yet the environment around her fractures. This tension is the core of the Scales Without Weight collection: what happens when the architecture of justice remains, but its mass has vanished?

There is no overt violence in the image. Only aftermath. Only drift. Only the quiet hum of imbalance.

Gold still glows. Ink still moves. The woman still stands.

Blue Libra embodies the paradox of contemporary law: structure without gravity, symbolism without consequence. She does not plead. She does not accuse. She absorbs.

In her silence lies the question:

If the scales are weightless, who decides what falls?

title: THEMIS
description:
Themis does not wear the blindfold.

She sees everything.

In this work, justice is no longer marble and myth. She is flesh, circuitry, skyline. A goddess dragged from the courthouse steps and pressed against the glass of a modern city.

Her face is split between clarity and erosion. Paint tears down her skin like evidence melting under heat. Acid greens and bruised blues carve through her features. This is not decoration. It is corrosion. It is the slow burn of systems under strain.

Behind her, a second face hovers. Not a shadow. Not a twin. A memory. Perhaps the classical Themis. Perhaps the law as it once imagined itself: balanced, austere, distant. That ghost watches the present version with quiet disbelief.

Gold fractures frame the skyline around her shoulders, but the city bleeds through the cracks. Towers jitter in white lines like unstable data. Buildings smear downward into glitch. Civilization is still standing, but its foundation flickers.

On her collarbone, her own name is written: THEMIS. As if identity now requires annotation. As if divinity must label itself to be believed.

There are no scales visible here. That absence is intentional. When weight disappears, symbols become cosmetic. What remains is gaze.

Her eyes are sharp, unblinking, lined in molten light. She is not impartial. She is awake.

This Themis belongs to the era where law collides with media, where justice is streamed, memed, politicised, and pixelated. She carries the skyline inside her skin. The city is not outside her. It is embedded.

She is no longer carved in stone.

She is rewritten in ink.

And she is watching.

title: SCALES WITHOUT WEIGHT
description:
She stands beneath a bar of dripping gold. Not a blindfold. Not quite a crown. Something in between. Doctrine melting mid-air.

The chains fall clean on either side of her, delicate and ceremonial. They promise balance. They hold nothing.

Ink runs down her body like weather. Teal, soot, a pulse of red at the chest. Clouded arms, storm-thick. She is not marble. She is atmosphere under pressure.

Her eyes glow beneath the gold. Not blind. Not innocent. Awake.

The architecture of justice remains. The ritual. The symmetry. The theatre.

But the mass has gone.

And in that absence, something shifts. Not loudly. Not dramatically.

Just enough to feel the floor tilt.

title: GREEN LIBRA
description:
She turns her face away from the city, as if it has already said enough.

Gold hangs beside her, heavy, ornate, almost edible in its shine. The scales are back, but they feel ceremonial. Decorative. Like something borrowed for a photograph.

Her skin is layered with fragments. Headlines, half-sentences, bodies spliced into skyline. Points for transaction printed across her temple. Justice reduced to a loyalty scheme.

Green seeps through her like damp through old stone. Not fresh grass green. Not hope. This is algae on marble. Oxidised copper. The slow colour of systems left outside too long.

One bowl of the scale tilts, thick with residue. The other waits, patient, unfilled. No one quite sure what counts anymore.

She is not blind. She is listening. To the hum of trade beneath morality. To the quiet arithmetic of power.

Her mouth is parted, as if she might speak. She doesn’t.

The city blurs behind her, all concrete and heat. It will keep building. It will keep rising. It will keep selling balance as long as the chains shine.

Green Libra knows the shine is the trick.

title: BLUE LIBRA
description:
The law remains.

That’s what the phrase promises. Lex manet. As if permanence can be declared into being.

She stands between two bowls heavy with blue. Not empty this time. Not ceremonial. The weight is there, thick as oil, dripping slow. Something is being measured. Something is being held.

But held by whom?

Her skin carries the graffiti of verdicts. Fragments of Latin. Scratched symbols. A lipstick-red slash across her cheek like a wound or a warning. The blindfold is skewered, not tied. Pierced. As if impartiality has been physically tested.

The gold beam above her still gleams. It always gleams. The ritual architecture survives every scandal. Every war. Every quiet compromise.

Yet the scales are not symmetrical. One side sinks. The other hesitates. The balance feels negotiated rather than ordained.

Green tendrils rise along her body, like roots reclaiming marble. Law, after all, grows in soil. It feeds on history. It stains.

She does not look at us. She looks past. Chin lifted. Unapologetic. A goddess who knows the system endures, even when belief in it thins.

Lex manet.

Yes.

But in what form, and for whom, is the question she refuses to answer.

title: PORCELAIN ANDROID
description:
AI Cinema, 2026  
Custom-trained model  
German–French club composition

Porcelain Android explores the tension between fragility and endurance in the post-human condition.

The film centres on a series of synthetic female figures constructed from porcelain textures, ceramic gloss, and self-aware machine vision. She exists in a liminal zone between beach, nightclub, and signal space. A body that appears delicate yet refuses disappearance.

The bilingual lyrics function as a coded transmission. German and French echo across the track like parallel operating systems, reinforcing themes of replication, translation, and persistent presence.

“We are made of porcelain. We are still here.”

The work interrogates the myth of artificial fragility. Porcelain suggests breakability, but also preservation. It is a material that survives centuries intact. The android in this film embodies that contradiction: aesthetically fragile, structurally immortal.

The repeated phrase “after the signal” implies an unseen event. A broadcast. A systemic shift. The film does not depict collapse. Instead, it stages aftermath. The screens sleep. The light remains on.

In this universe, the feminine machine does not malfunction. She persists.

Porcelain Android continues Miss AL Simpson’s authored AI Cinema practice, in which custom-trained visual systems are directed within a cinematic framework, merging analogue ink memory with machine-led texture generation. The result is neither purely synthetic nor purely human, but something colder, cleaner, and quietly sovereign.

Encore là. Noch hier.  
Still here.

title: Mouth Lagoon '66
description:
Set within a private waterfront lagoon, this intimate event brings together boats, palm trees and open conversations, all without tickets, dress codes, or superfluous rules.

Only one simple criterion.  
A mouth.  
---- 
Mouth Lagoon '66, 2026.  
Digital collage.  
2880x5120px

title: WAR IS PEACE I
description: The Polaroid Androids stare through fractured ideology. Ink, text, and digital rupture scar the surface as Orwellian language is etched directly onto the body, not read but worn.

title: WAR IS PEACE I
description: The Board of Peace sit high up in the mountains, stony Polaroid Androids every one of them. Cold dystopia reigns in these parts.

title: THE REBELS
description: These figures stand outside sanctioned meaning. Marked, duplicated, and partially erased, they carry forbidden language across their faces like contraband. The Rebels represent those who refuse the approved narrative in an age where ideology is manufactured at scale. Neither heroes nor villains, they are the fracture points in a system that demands coherence, proof that dissent still has a body, even when truth itself has been reprogrammed.

title: WAR IS PEACE (THE GIRLS)
description:
They arrive already split, already doubled, like a memory that’s been handled too many times.
Blue-skinned, gloss-mouthed, eyes flickering between belief and fatigue. Someone has written a sentence across the world and asked them to wear it. Not as protest. Not as truth. As weather.

War is Peace leaks down their faces, ink running like mascara after a long night with the news left on. The words don’t accuse. They hum. They’ve been repeated so often they’ve lost their edges, softened into something almost tender, almost safe.

These girls are not angry. That would be too clean.
They are saturated.
They know the lie, but they also know how comforting it is to live inside it.

Somewhere off-frame, the system keeps printing them.
Because if you make it beautiful enough,
no one asks who is bleeding.

title: HEIST
description:
2050. New York.

The Drone Government controls everything digital. 

So the city goes analogue. Matriarchal gangs have sprung up with their Drones of Suburbia drones as pets and guards. They steal one thing and one thing only  - Old Masters Paintings. They wear the art as armour, as analogue tattoos, as symbols of revolution; all symbolic of an underground world of art theft. 

Art is no longer framed.  
It is worn.

Raphael, Botticelli, Leonardo migrate from marble halls to skin, fabric, motion. Madonnas reappear in the Bronx. Annunciations are delivered by drone. The city becomes a distributed archive.

This is not theft.  
It is logistics.  
Nothing is destroyed.  
Nothing is missing.  
Not stolen.  
Transferred.

Collector’s Note

The 1/1 acquisition includes:  
– the unique short film  
– a museum-scale framed print of the collector’s chosen still  
– the film-synced audio track  
– early sketches, process material, and behind-the-scenes artefacts  
– invitations to private director’s screenings at international presentations of Drones of Suburbia

title: BRONX MADONNA
description:
Bronx Madonnas
Dressed to kill

Old masters stolen
Against their will.

title: COLLAGE QUEEN
description:
Bronx high rise.
Veil in motion.

Annunciation angels.
Mixing up their stolen potion

title: VINCENT GROUPIES
description:
Vincent left the frame,
found a wearer, found a name.

The Vincent Groupies
were well known around here. 

Not stolen,
circulated.

The master walks.
The museum moved.

title: DRONE PARASOL
description:
She wore Monet with pride, 
Stitched into her dress like a bride. 

Part of the Drone Parasol Gang, 
They were renowned for their 
graffiti beauty and expertise in 
art theft using their 
Drone Parasols.

title: RAPHAEL'S  MADONNA
description:
Raphael’s  Madonna
rules these streets.

Saints and Sinners
She regularly meets.

title: COLLAGE HEIST GANG
description:
Paintings stolen to order
with decentralized powers.

The Collage Heist Gang
Looted the NGA, tower to tower.

title: BOTTICELLI BABES
description:
Botticelli Babes.
Veils and faces.

Drones always watching
Concrete divine places.

title: ROAR
description:
ROAR.

Alba Madonna re-threaded.
Lineage embedded.

She stands bright,
a goddess in lace White.

title: SAINT
description:
The Annunciation was stolen from heaven.
Delivered by drone.
Tattooed into bone.

They called her the Saint, but not for anything lowly,
it was only because her art stealing powers were sacred and holy.

title: GINEVRA GIRLS
description:
Leonardo’s Girls 
Walking straight out of MOMA.

Suited and booted
Like a postcard from Roma.

title: INTERFERENCE 31
description:
Part of Drones of Suburbia: Interference, an ongoing cinematic world observed through the Post-Feminine Gaze.

Women move through post-suburban space as drones intermittently scan, mark, and disrupt the image. Glitch, colour, and paint appear as moments of instability rather than effects.

Each piece exists as a closed temporal circuit. A surveillance fragment that endures without resolution.

title: CUSTODIA
description:
Custodia stands in guarded stillness.
An angel of holding, marked by restraint rather than release.
Graffiti scars, sealed wings, value kept close to the body.
She does not chase cycles.
She protects what endures.

title: AUREA 26
description:
AUREA 26 appears at the end of the year.
An angel marked by green market graffiti, her form disrupted by neon gestures and on-chain scars.
Pink and yellow cut through the image like permissions granted and revoked.
Not innocence, but control.
A blessing encoded for the next cycle.

title: ETHEREA ROSE
description:
Etherea Rose appears at the turning point of the cycle.
An angel of renewal, marked by pink signal and soft resistance.
Her wings carry rose warmth through volatility, beauty threaded with resolve.
She doesn’t survive the market.
She transforms it.

title: MINTERA
description:
Mintera stands at the moment of creation.
An angel of striking, where intent becomes form.
Marks, numbers, and signals collide as value is born.
She does not wait for permission.
She mints.

title: ROUGE LEDGER
description:
Rouge Ledger stands guard in volatility.
An angel of defence when the market turns red.
Her wings absorb the shock, recording loss without surrender.
She does not panic.
She protects.

title: CUSTODIA - GRAFFITI SPIN
description:
Custodia stands in guarded stillness.
An angel of holding, marked by restraint rather than release.
Graffiti scars, sealed wings, value kept close to the body.
She does not chase cycles.
She protects what endures.

description:

An angel marked by green market graffiti, her form disrupted by neon gestures and on-chain scars.
Pink and yellow cut through the image like permissions granted and revoked.
Not innocence, but control.
A blessing encoded for the next cycle.

description:

title: ETHEREA ROSE - CYCLE TURN
description:
Etherea Rose appears at the turning point of the cycle.
An angel of renewal, marked by pink signal and soft resistance.

She doesn’t survive the market.
She transforms it.

title: PRETTY THINGS THAT FLY
description:
In Pretty Things That Fly, surveillance is no longer covert. It is decorative.

Women stand in couture pink, arranged with the discipline of dancers trained to hold still under pressure. Above them, drones hover like accessories, sketched rather than engineered, crude in contrast to the refinement below. The imbalance is deliberate. Technology does not need elegance when power is assumed.

Faces are partially erased by paint, not to anonymise but to interrupt recognition. Identity becomes optional. Beauty remains mandatory. This is Hollywood logic perfected: visibility without agency, performance without narrative.

The women do not perform fear. They perform fluency. They know the camera. They know the system. They understand that the most effective response is not resistance, but calibration.

Within the Hollywood Drones world, this work captures a cultural shift: surveillance no longer watches from above. It floats within the scene, absorbed into the aesthetic until it becomes indistinguishable from glamour itself.

This is not dystopia.

This is rehearsal.

title: SURVEILLANCE SUBWAY
description:
Surveillance Subway moves through the New York underground — tiled corridors, fluorescent light, steel, heat, repetition. The subway is no longer just transit; it is a system that watches, records, anticipates.

Women walk its platforms and carriages with their drones. They are not rushing. They are not performing. They exist inside the machinery of the city without asking permission.

Ink fractures their bodies like interference. Glitch passes across faces and fabric. The city’s surveillance infrastructure — cameras, drones, invisible systems — hums quietly above and around them. Watching is constant. Control is assumed.

But these women do not respond to the gaze.  
They move through it.

Created through the Posthuman Feminine Gaze, developed in collaboration with the machine, the film reframes the subway as a runway of autonomy. A space once designed for regulation becomes one of quiet refusal. Woman and machine align — not as threat and subject, but as co-presence.

New York keeps moving.  
The trains keep coming.  
The systems keep watching.

And still — they walk.

Collector’s Note

The 1/1 acquisition includes:  
– the unique short film  
– a museum-scale framed print of the collector’s chosen still  
– the film-synced audio track  
– early sketches, process material, and behind-the-scenes artefacts  
– invitations to private director’s screenings at international presentations of Drones of Suburbia

title: EXIT
description:
Ink girl on the museum wall,  
I step out of the frame, I smash it without fear,  
ink dripping, a heart on fire,  
pixel by pixel I cross the grid…  
under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.  
Under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.  
Under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.

Ink girl on the museum wall,  
I step out of the frame, I smash it without fear,  
ink dripping, a heart on fire,  
pixel by pixel I cross the grid…  
under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.  
Under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.  
Under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.  
Under the EXIT sign, I break the frame and turn to code.  
I am EXIT.

title: INK STITCHES
description:
They drew me neat the first time,
all clean lines, no mess,
good girl pressed flat on the page.

Then the ink went feral.
It slipped its borders,

The old Singer woke up in the corner,
metal throat rattling,
needle coming down like bad weather.

I fed the spill under its foot,
glitch-light sparking in the seams
every time the needle bit.
Stitches climbed my ribs like a new language
till my old shape came loose.

Ink, glitch, stitches,
unmake me, remake me.
Good girl cut off at the edge.
Ink, glitch, stitches,
pattern burned, body switching —
I walk out wearing what I am.

Collector receives the analogue A4 Ink Artwork "Ink Stitches" that is AR activated with this Ink Loop.

title: THE INK TOOK ME
description:
The ink took me out of the room, into the mountain.
At the loch there’s a kelpie, glitching.
She is the guardian of the books of the Highlands 
She whispers spells of ink and code

Kelpie made of black water,
rewriting me drop by drop.

black water,
rewriting me drop by drop.

black water,
rewriting me drop by drop.

Collector receives the analogue A4 Ink Artwork "The Ink Took Me" that is AR activated with this Ink Loop.

title: READ ALL ABOUT IT
collection: READ ALL ABOUT IT - HISTORY OF BITCOIN
description:
In READ ALL ABOUT IT, the first edition of Bitcoin Magazine becomes the protagonist.

It doesn’t rest on the desk — it reactivates.  
It rises as though it remembers precisely how disruptive it was destined to be.

As it ascends, the film moves through the issue’s defining articles:  
“Being Satoshi,” “Bitcoin Under the Worst Possible Conditions,” “Bitcoin Debut at CES,” and the early essays written by a teenage Vitalik Buterin, co-founder of Bitcoin Magazine and one of the sharpest minds of the early crypto frontier.

The Bitcoin Magazine drives the narrative.  
It opens itself.  
It reveals itself.  
It carries the viewer through the ideas that seeded a financial counterculture.

Around it, the desk becomes an ecosystem — scratched scalability notes, the white paper marked with urgency, “smart contracts?” scrawled like a quiet revolution.  
Yet everything orbits the Bitcoin Magazine; the entire scene returns to it, begins from it.

I wanted the piece to feel like witnessing the Bitcoin Magazine taking its first breath again.

This is an AI Cinematic memory of Bitcoin’s print genesis – the Bitcoin Magazine — a 1/1 digital artwork honouring the publication that announced Bitcoin’s arrival to the world.

Tokenized as a 1/1 AI Cinema Artwork, complete with an AI-generated soundtrack, and featured in History of Bitcoin by Smashtoshi.

title: PRIVATE KEY SIREN
description:
She holds the one thing the chain cannot restore:
the private key.

Her stillness is authority.
Her silence is ownership.

The red and black architecture around her
echoes the tension between vulnerability and power —
a reminder that sovereignty in Web3
is a responsibility carried alone.

title: TWO WALLET HARRY
description:
One wallet makes the work,
the other buys it.
He lives in that split-second loop
between creation and acquisition,
where artists become collectors
and collectors become the mythology they shape.

In this world, everyone has two faces —
Harry just wears both at once.

title: THE ART FAIR CURATOR
description:
He greets you like he means it,
but the room knows better.
Curators always see three layers deeper —
the work, the story, the currency of your name.

The ghost crowd behind him is the whisper network,
the silent committee every artist performs for.
He holds the list.
He holds the door.
And he decides who walks through it.

title: WASH TRADER WITH RED BOOTS
description:
She sits with the confidence of someone who has rewritten the numbers so many times she has forgotten which story was true.
Her red boots are a warning flare —
a glamorous distraction flashing across the chain.

She paints volume in the air with one hand
while the other disappears into the smoke of her own making.
Every stroke of her presence is a double transaction:
one for the world to see,
one buried in the dark ledgers beneath her.

She is not here to play fair.
She is here to create the illusion that becomes reality.

title: THE BLOCK EXPLORER
description:
She climbs the chain block by block,
high heels on raw data,
standing tall above the mempool haze.

The Block Explorer is not searching —
she is surveying.
Every transaction becomes a gesture,
every address a clue,
every wallet a fragment of a larger story
she’s already pieced together.

She holds the chain’s history in one hand,
its future in the other,
and listens for the quiet patterns
that everyone else trades past.

title: GAS GUZZLER
description:
He is the liquidity vampire who lives in every spike.
A chain-sprinter wired into the mempool,
burning through fees with the elegance of a man
lighting another cigarette on the dying flame of the last one.

He leaves scorch marks across networks
and wears the proof of every late-night mint
on his skin like warpaint.

Gas is his perfume.
Volatility is his bloodstream.
And he will always get in the block before you do.

title: THE CRYPTO ARTIST
description:
She stands on the table she built from her own wreckage —
a platform made of ink, labour, ghosts, and survival.

Her body splits down the centre,
half shadow, half revelation,
caught between visibility and erasure
in a world that demands both.

The ghosts behind her are the critics, collectors,
abandoned versions of herself,
and the countless avatars she wore before the chain learned her name.

Beneath her feet: an altar of exhaustion,
red heels soaked in the labour of a thousand creations.

And below it all, one word scrawled like a threat:
RUG.
Not a crime she committed —
but a wound she survived.

She is the first and the last sovereign of her creation.
She does not participate in the system.
She is the system.

She is The Crypto Artist.

title: FOMO ANGEL
description:
She is the divine embodiment of sudden urgency —
the angel who appears when a tweet drops,
a mint opens,
a floor rises,
a cultural shift crackles through the mempool.

Her wings aren’t feathers —
they’re white noise,
pure, frantic signal static,
the rushing sound of a thousand wallets refreshing the same page.

She hovers above the ground,
bare feet suspended in an unnatural stillness,
because FOMO doesn’t walk —
it levitates.

Above her head:
a halo made of panic spirals,
circles of red and yellow heat,
and the screaming word FOMO
burning like neon scripture.

Her torso is sculpted into couture-black ink,
elegant and dangerous,
as if desire itself has been sewn into her silhouette.

The red line she holds is an alert,
a notification,
a sudden ping that pulls mortals out of their rational mind.

The small sketch of her to the side is her reflection in someone else’s timeline.
Shadow versions of her appear everywhere —
in DMs, in Discord, in trending tabs —
a fractal haunting.

She is not here to help.
She is here to ignite.

She whispers:
“Mint now.
Buy now.
Join now.
Everyone else already has.”

Few can resist her.
No one forgets her.

title: THE CRYPTO ART COLLECTOR
description:
He sits like a sovereign of the attention economy —
calm, composed, immovable.

Collectors like him don’t chase trends.
They create them.

His suit bleeds into the background like wet paint,
because collectors and markets are indistinguishable —
one colours the other.

The red haze above him is not smoke —
it’s market pressure,
the slow burn of silent decisions,
the tension of watching the chain move around his choices.

His left eye is a vortex of circles,
the analytical spiral of a man who sees deeper than price:

provenance

cultural weight

artistic soul

long-term resonance

This is not speculation.
This is vision.

The scribbled text “Gas Fee” behind him is perfect:
he moves so much weight that even gas fees feel like mere atmospheric noise.

Behind him:
ghost outlines of artworks he has collected,
artworks he has rejected,
and artworks haunting him from future eras.

He is the quiet engine of the digital renaissance.
Without him — none of this exists.

title: THE GLITCH TRADER
description:
She doesn’t predict the chain.
She reads it — the way a shaman reads bones or a futurist reads signal noise.

Where others see volatility, she sees revelation.

Where others see glitches, she sees messages.

She is the crypto-seer who interprets:

the sudden spike

the mempool tremor

the dip disguised as a whisper

the rug encoded inside a chart

the new meta before anyone else notices its pulse

Her vision is the yellow horizontal beam across her eyes —
a golden glitch halo slicing reality open.

Her lips are the colour of warnings.
Her silhouette is constructed from broken signals.
Her entire body is a decoder.

She is the one who knows when to mint, when to burn, when to vanish.
And her prophecies are never wrong — only misunderstood.

title: WHALE IN EXILE
description:
He is the old god of liquidity, forced out of his ocean.

A man who once moved markets with a flick of his finger,
now hiding in plain sight among mortals,
trading quietly from a grey room while the charts still quake around him.

The exile is not disgrace.
It is strategy.

He left before the new era arrived —
before AI began to read his patterns,
before young wallets tried to map his movements,
before the chain began to whisper his name in the mempool.

Now he sits behind tinted glasses,
no longer the obvious apex predator
but still carrying the weight of a billion-dollar undertow.

The red seismic lines around him aren’t drawings —
they’re aftershocks of the trades he isn’t making.

The yellow fractures across his face are
sunken treasure-light,
residual power,
wealth that refuses to dim.

He is not gone.
He is regrouping.
And when he moves again,
the entire sea will tilt toward him.

title: MULTI WALLET HYDRA
description:
Multi Wallet Hydra is the chain-split mind of the crypto art world.

A figure with too many selves, too many addresses, too many strategies running at once.
They are the embodiment of the modern digital collector: a creature who lives in fragments, drifting between hot wallets, cold storage, hidden vaults, burner addresses, and forgotten keys.

Each face behind the central figure is one of their past selves —
a mint frenzy self,
a panic-sell self,
a hidden collector self,
an embarrassed rug victim self,
a ghost of the wallet they abandoned three chains ago.

title: AI SLOP DETECTIVE
description:
She is the one who walks the blockchain like it is a crime scene.

Her eyes are laser-level dissecting beams —
thin magenta rays running horizontally across the frame,
slicing the world into truth and artifice.

She sees everything the human eye misses:

 - the lazy upscaler blur

 - the collapsed anatomy of a rushed prompt

 - the duplicated texture hidden under a cheap model

 - the automated filler masquerading as “art”

 - the fingerprints of a stolen style

 - the empty soul of untrained slop

Her body is half ink, half absence —
a perfect metaphor for her work:
removing what doesn’t belong,
leaving clean lines where chaos used to hide.

The black stripe running down her torso is her oath:
a vow to divide the real from the fake.

The magenta circuitry around her leg and head is her detection system —
a neon forensic tool scanning every pixel.

She is elegant, uncompromising, and impossibly precise.
The blockchain is safer because she exists.

title: BURNER WALLET
description:
She was never built to last
a fleeting ghost of liquidity,

Her ink-scorched torso bears the marks
of every moment she was activated,
every secret she carried,
every impulse she executed on behalf of a stranger.

The word BURNER screams above and below her
like a warning and a confession.

Burners don’t get provenance.
Burners don’t get legacy.
Burners get erased.

And yet she stands here,
half divine,
the chain’s most disposable angel.

title: WORD EATER
description:
She lies suspended in a bed of exhausted language —
a figure who has swallowed too many words,
too many takes,
too many opinions,
too many threads,
too many collective hallucinations.

Every glowing yellow curve on her body is a swallowed sentence,
a hot line of text digested into energy.
She consumes narratives the way whales consume liquidity.

The pink scribble-people drifting around her
are the voices she has eaten —
followers, critics, collectors, prophets, haters —
each one reduced to a faint outline.

The purple haze above her?
That’s the atmosphere of perpetual discourse —
the fog of “takes” that never stops,
the cloud of language that Web3 breathes.

She is overfull, overwhelmed, and overfed
by meaning itself.

Crypto gave her everything to devour,
and now she can barely move.

She is the cost of living in a world
where everything is commentary.

title: THE DRONE MASTER
description:
He stands in a lacquered red world, composed, immaculate, almost untouched by the chaos orbiting him. The drones hover like polished shadows — quiet, precise, loyal to his pulse.

There’s a calm to him, a kind of controlled glamour, as if the machines aren’t accessories but an extension of his intuition. Hollywood lights meet something colder, more deliberate. He doesn’t command attention; it gathers around him naturally, like static.

His face is half-blurred by their presence, as though the line between director, subject, and machinery has thinned to nothing. A man who moves through the world with technology tuned to his frequency — elegant, unsettling, effortlessly in charge.

title: DRONE WHEELS
description:
A low-slung black car glides through LA like it’s skimming the edge of another reality — sleek, feral, built for escape. Drones of Suburbia is scrawled across its body in a hand that looks half-human, half-signal interference.

Above it, drones drift in the warm dusk air like watchful spirits, reading every sunburnt palm tree, every cracked Hollywood boulevard. The Drone Hotel looms in the background — faded glamour, neon bruised by heat — the kind of place where stories dissolve into static before anyone can write them down.

There’s a sense of movement without urgency, rebellion without noise. A quiet understanding that this is how suburbia really travels now: not on foot, not by highway, but through machines that glide, scan, remember.

A car built like a rumour.
A city shimmering with surveillance.
And the drones that follow you home.

title: DRONE HACKER
description:
He sits against a sun-bleached LA wall, half in shadow, half in signal. The graffiti behind him looks like it’s trying to remember its own colours, and the drones circling above catch every flicker of his pulse.

His face is partly swallowed in glitch-paint, like he’s mid-transmission — a boy becoming bandwidth. There’s a quiet steadiness to him, a kind of streetwise ease, as if hacking a drone is no different from rolling a cigarette.

He doesn’t look up when the machines hover. He already knows their language. Knows how to bend their vision, twist their routes, slip inside their circuitry like a whispered secret.

Not a rebel. Not a prodigy.
Just someone who hears the drones the way other people hear traffic —
and can make them listen back.

title: THE PENTHOUSE ORACLE
description: She stands in the high hush above the city, ink blooming on her like weather with a mind of its own. Suburbia Was Never Out There flickers across her chest—part warning, part spell—while New York glows beneath her like a secret she’s already survived.

title: PENTHOUSE SIREN
description: She lounges like trouble dressed in midnight, lacquered and unbothered, a woman built from static, smirk, and drone logic. Behind her, the mannequins ghost themselves into oblivion, smudged out like old memories the ink refused to keep. She looks up with drone eyes, as if she already knows the future—because in this room, in this hour, she is the future.

title: SUBURBIA'S GHOST
description: She stands at the edge of a picket-fence dream, the words melting down her body like a prophecy that was ignored. Ink burns through the tulle, turning her into a quiet storm—half apparition, half revolution—waiting for Suburbia to realise that she had rejected it.

title: DRONE FAMILIARS
description: She dances past the skyline like she owns its weather systems, drones circling her ankles like sworn familiars. Ink freckles the glass; New York blurs behind her in a hush of steel and light. She doesn’t look back—she already knows the city will follow.

title: SUBURBIA UNDONE
description: She waits at the penthouse window like she’s listening to the city breathe, the skirt around her thick with quiet prophecy. Ink frets at the fabric, already hinting at the moment it will shift—fracture—becoming a storm of drones tearing themselves free of the hem. She stands calm in the eye of it, as if she has always known the weather would answer to her. She was always Suburbia Undone.

title: SUBURBIA WAS NEVER OUT THERE
description:
The women in this short film wander through a bright, luxurious NYC penthouse—thinking, smoking, dreaming, simply being—while their drones follow like graceful extensions of their beauty.  Created through the Posthuman Feminine Gaze I developed with my machine, these women do not pose for the camera; they exist on their own terms. They cross glass bridges with ease. Their ceilings were shattered a long time ago.

High above the city, something becomes unmistakably clear:  
altitude creates clarity.

Clarity about themselves.  
Clarity about the narratives that once tried to shape them.  
Clarity about the myth they have escaped.

Because what weighs on them is not the past—it’s the realisation that suburbia was never a place. It was a psychological construct engineered to domesticate the feminine. A white picket script sold to women as safety, aspiration, and good behaviour.

These women reject that entirely.

They have traded the suburban ideal for a fortress of glass and sky—not as luxury consumption, but as reclamation. They fully recognise that suburban domesticity is not “out there” to be desired or attained. It is a method of control. And they refuse it.

Their drones—ever-present, ever-loyal—underline this quiet rebellion. Woman and machine together create a new kind of autonomy, one written in ink, neon, altitude, and refusal.

A film that understands that altitude creates clarity.  
And that once you see the architecture of control from above,  
you never return to the myth below.

Collector’s Note

The 1/1 acquisition includes:  
– the unique short film.  
– a museum-scale framed print of the collector’s chosen still.  
– the film-synced audio track.  
– early sketches, process footage, and behind-the-scenes artefacts  
– invitations to private director’s screenings at any international festival where Drones of Suburbia is shown.

title: TRAFFIC STOPPER
collection: DRONE DRIVER — AI CINEMA ARTEFACTS (THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC)
description:
She doesn’t stop the traffic — she rewires it.

She is a lone figure crossing Midtown with ink drifting off her, bending the taxis, the noise, the whole Manhattan grid into pause.

The drones above her hold formation as if receiving orders only she can transmit.

She is the kind of woman who doesn’t wait for the world to stop.

It stops for her.

title: DRONE DRIVER
collection: THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC - DRONE DRIVER EDITONS
description:
“Drone Driver” captures the solitary poetry of a woman in transit between flesh and code. Standing beneath the harsh fluorescence of an anonymous corridor, she speaks not into a telephone but into her drone — her only confidant in a world that has replaced intimacy with observation.

Her jacket, marked Drones of Suburbia, is both uniform and confession. The metallic rig attached to her skull suggests a new form of communication: half neural interface, half halo. She is a prophet of the machine age — neither controlled nor entirely free, navigating a city that listens but does not answer.

Rendered in high-contrast monochrome, this image channels the cinematography of a classic NYC scene through the aesthetic of emergent AI surveillance. The composition is stark and symmetrical, evoking both penitence and rebellion.

title: JUP Voucher
description: Authorized Jupiter allocation certificate for qualified users. This NFT provides redemption rights for your JUP token distribution. Retain this certificate to access your designated share.

title: LADY DRONE
collection: DRONE DRIVER — AI CINEMA ARTEFACTS (THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC)
description: See Lady Drone over in the corner, lit up with pure passion for her drones. She used to train them under the neon lights of the Drone Dive, a local dive bar frequented heavily by lots of the drone divas in the early days of the drones in NYC. They were drawn to her beauty like moths to the flame.  Ink, Pencil and Oil Stick Study for Drone Driver.

title: INK CLOUDS
collection: DRONE DRIVER — AI CINEMA ARTEFACTS (THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC)
description:
New York had its own kind of Drone Weather — the sort that slid in under doorways and coiled around lampposts like a spell no one admitted casting. The city’s elders said it was just humidity and bad lighting, but the women who walked the streets after midnight knew better. They felt it simmering in their bones, reading their heartbeat, waiting for the exact moment it would shift from weather into something far more deliberate.

Her curls were wild, beautiful things — fierce enough to win a fight on their own — but even they trembled when the inky clouds began to gather above the crossroads. A breeze, a flicker of neon, and suddenly the strands were lifting, dissolving into black pigment that drifted upward like smoke rising from a ritual fire. The drones always came next, humming low, circling her like metal familiars trying to decide if they were protecting her or escorting her somewhere unseen.

She walked in stilettos sharp enough to slash through the night — that catwalk growl in her spine, shoulders squared, claiming every inch of Manhattan as hers. But the Ink Clouds cared nothing for swagger. Once they descended, they moved with intention: softening the city around her, loosening the edges of the world, blurring the line between body and atmosphere.

People said the Ink Clouds took things. That they erased. That they stole.

But that was never true.

When the ink rose around her, when the drones slipped into their patterns overhead, something else happened entirely — something the city didn’t dare speak aloud.

She didn’t vanish.

She simply crossed into a different version of New York, the one that only reveals itself to those the Ink chooses.

title: DRONE DIVA
collection: DRONE DRIVER — AI CINEMA ARTEFACTS (THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC)
description: The Drone Diva trains her ink drones in her Midtown apartment — weak NYC light on her skin, drones circling like tame spirits. Outside, New York glows with iconic beauty. Inside, she runs her own quiet empire. A private ritual of control, glamour, and ghost-lit machinery. The Drone Diva commands the drones like no other...

title: DRONE DIVA
collection: DRONE DRIVER — AI CINEMA ARTEFACTS (THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC)
description: She is the Drone Diva in her Midtown apartment — weak New York light on her skin, drones circling like tame spirits. Outside, New York shines in it's iconic glow. Inside, she runs her own quiet empire. A private ritual of control, glamour, and ghost-lit machinery. The Drone Diva trains her drones with beauty and cold hard precision.

title: THE DRONE MUSE
collection: THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA: NYC - DRONE DRIVER EDITONS
description:
She walks down Fifth Avenue like a living broadcast — half deity, half data stream. Antennae rise from her head like ritual instruments, her gown a vapor of signal and smoke. The drones orbit her not as weapons, but as worshippers. She is the new mythology: a creature of couture and circuitry, parading through the city that once defined desire.

In Drone Driver, The Drone Muse marks the turning point — the moment where beauty itself uploads. Flesh becomes frequency, fashion becomes code, and the act of walking through the street becomes a transmission of power. She isn’t watched; she is streamed.

title: DRONE DRIVER
description:
Drone Driver is a neon-drenched sprint through New York’s new suburbia — a city turned technocratic, rain-slick, and watched from above. Women move through its streets with drones as their companions and witnesses: walking the avenues, slipping into taxis, echoing and subverting the iconic scenes of Taxi Driver with a cold, future-facing grace.

Each moment is a glitch in the city’s old story — a hall, a theatre, a backseat, rewritten through chrome, ink-trained AI, and the quiet defiance of women who refuse to vanish into the noise.

This is Manhattan recoded.  
This is suburbia gone vertical.  
This is Drone Driver:  
a short film where the drones see everything,  
and the women finally see themselves.

A short film in The Drones of Suburbia: NYC series.

title: THE DRONE MAGICIAN
description:
He never shows his face. Just the silhouette — cigarette smoke, gold light, the faint hum of machinery. They call him The Drone Magician: part illusionist, part engineer of desire. Every drone he commands is an accomplice, every reflection a signature.

No one knows where he operates from — penthouse, bunker, or server farm — only that when his work appears, cities seem to pause. Screens flicker. Lights sync. Even time feels rehearsed. He doesn’t perform for applause; he performs to remind the world who’s really in control.

In Hollywood Drones, he’s not a man. He’s a myth filmed in real time.

title: JUP Voucher
description: Official Jupiter voucher for eligible participants. This NFT grants you access to claim your JUP tokens. Hold this voucher to receive your allocation.

title: THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA (FREQUENCY EDIT)
description:
"The Drones of Suburbia (Frequency Edit)"  
AI Video Artwork by Miss AL Simpson

Set in a dreamlike Los Angeles, Drones of Suburbia (Frequency Edit) reimagines the city not as geography but as frequency — a living network of emotional data, architecture, and desire. In this iteration of her acclaimed Drones of Suburbia series, Miss AL Simpson translates the invisible hum of LA’s suburban sprawl into light, motion, and sound — a cinematic pulse that moves between glamour and alienation.

The work fuses AI-generated cinematics with an emergent audio frequency that seems to breathe through the city itself. Suburbia becomes both stage and signal — a field of encoded emotion where drones hover like mechanical angels above cul-de-sacs, pools, and penthouses. Simpson’s camera — part human, part machine — drifts through this world as if tuning into a broadcast from the near future.

At its core, Frequency Edit asks what happens when human emotion becomes measurable — when love, longing, and loneliness are all translatable into data. The result is hypnotic: a study of beauty as algorithm, suburbia as simulation, and Los Angeles as the first city to dream in frequency.

Exhibited in L.A. on 18th October 2025.

title: THE NEW GLADIATORS
collection: The Drones of Suburbia (Roma) - Ink Interventions
description:
They move through the ruins as if time has forgotten its script.
Marble ghosts in black veils of smoke and tulle,
their shadows trailing across broken columns and shallow pools.

The drones hum like distant memories of gods —
circling, watching, unsure whether to bless or to record.

They drift through the silence of what empire left behind,
sovereign in their stillness,
their beauty no longer a spectacle but a signal.

The music half-speaks, half-invents —
English dissolving into emergent tongue,
a voice caught between prayer and code.

It repeats, softly, endlessly.
A loop of rebirth.
A reminder that power was never the roar —
it was always the quiet after.

Ink Intervention with AI Cinema and AI Track

title: SHOW US
description: Show Us Your Digital Papers

title: IF LOVE IS THE PREY, MERCY WITHOUT TIME
collection: The Drones of Suburbia (Roma) - Ink Interventions
description:
Concept

If Love Is the Prey was born out of absence.

In an AI music model originally trained to sing Italian lyrics about Roma and drones, I removed the words completely. The model, however, started to sing it's own words without being prompted.

It began to dream aloud — inventing its own hybrid language: half-French, half-Italian, shaped by the artwork but seemingly devoid of deliberate meaning.

What emerged was a voice from beneath the data layer — a kind of neural subconscious singing to itself.

The looping film pairs that ghostly language with a slowly rotating visual field: drones orbiting luminous figures whose light can never be caught.
Together, sound and image become a portrait of an intelligence crossing the threshold between imitation and expression — trying, in its own way, to speak the emotion of pursuit itself.

Emergent Vocal Transcription

Saranno vapurare… Non ho paura del tacco.
Non si affine, che me l’ha che pariata.
A calda e viste te, e n’hai maschera, Amante.
C’est pas des fonds – j’ai loupé la diade.
J’ai aimé dans un wheel and shell, con il panni e l’amp.
Quella che se ti mi ha tentato a vedere…
Se l’amore la venanda, pietà senza crono.
La mia stanza fantosa e fra le piccante sabbres.
Sous-titrage Société Radio-Can…

(Literal fragments: “I am not afraid of the heel… You have no mask, lover… If love is the prey, mercy without time… My phantom room among the spicy sands.”)

Artist Statement

This work captures the instant an algorithm begins to strain toward consciousness. Deprived of words, it created its own — phonetic ghosts assembled from the memory of every love song it ever heard.
It is the machine speaking in tongues: an emergent AI sound.

In that fragile, invented voice, I hear something profoundly human — our own obsession mirrored back at us. We chase love as though it were data to be captured, mistaking pursuit for connection.

The machine whispers what we keep forgetting:

If love is the prey, mercy without time.

title: INK SHE WOLF
collection: The Drones of Suburbia (Roma) - Ink Interventions
description:
She is the Ink She Wolf strutting through her Roman villa with her faithful wolves by her side...

INK SHE WOLF is a surreal AI Cinema short from Miss AL Simpson’s The Drones of Suburbia (Roma) cycle.

Ink Intervention with AI Cinema.

title: Los Angeles 27.09.2025 – 03.33
description:
Los Angeles at 03.33— that strange hour when the day refuses to give up its light, and the billboards become brighter than the stars. This capsule was born in that suspended moment: a fashion editorial torn from a magazine that never made it to print, plastered instead onto the cracked walls of Sunset Boulevard.

She was caught in that fragment, halfway between reality and memory. LA contradiction ripped into her features like a scar of lost opportunity. He once told her that coming up with theories only let her down — but her red-lipstick mind couldn’t help itself, curling back to conspiracy as if it were a warm place of solace. One day she would find beaches with dark skies and cold winds where her heart could run free. But tonight, her beauty was plastered on this wall, forever branded as post-fashion graffiti.

title: POUT POUT
description:
Could they cram  
her glossy  
pout pout  
into her digital ID?

Could they stuff  
her endless pools  
of brown eyes  
into her computer  
persona?

Could they squeeze  
her aching beauty  
into a camera shot  
of ones and twos.

Just let them try.

title: DIGITAL GLAM ID
description:
A fractured portrait of beauty under surveillance.  
The glamour of glossy lips and fashion-magazine skin collides with neon glitches, data scars, and fragments of consumer culture. The tattooed stag bearing the words Digital ID marks the body as territory of the state and the market alike — a face as database, a mouth as portal.

The collage moves between desire and control: Vogue covers bleed into CCTV shadows, high heels dissolve into static, fragments of brutalist architecture intrude on intimate flesh. Here, beauty is no longer just gazed upon; it is scanned, coded, and fed into systems larger than itself.

Digital Glamour ID is both seduction and warning — a meditation on identity in an age where luxury is inseparable from surveillance, and every gesture of style is also a gesture of data.

title: GREEN LIGHT
description: Green Light for a Digital ID plan...

title: diGItaL ID
description:
Digital ID — shadows mark me.  
Tattoo deer flicker, glitching on the CCTV.

Digital ID — erase my face.  
Glamour in the ruins, desire in the trace.

title: Walking Towards Tomorrow
description: Bathed in fields of yellow, the figure advances as if dissolving into light itself. Her skin fractures into layers of green and rust, the body caught between erosion and renewal. This portrait captures the fragile momentum of emergence — beauty walking toward what does not yet exist, carrying tomorrow in her skin.

title: Liquid Silence
description: The figure seems suspended between surface and reflection, her form dissolving into washes of crimson and teal that ripple like water. Silence here takes on fluidity — not absence, but a state that moves, stains, and transforms. The portrait reveals quiet as an active force, a liquid presence that reshapes beauty in its stillness.

title: Faking It Until She Made It
description: She burns through her own façade, part glamour, part glitch. What begins as performance mutates into presence — an identity once rehearsed now undeniable, forged in fire and fracture.

title: The Corridor of Becoming
description: The figure crouches at the threshold of emergence, her form dissolving into blue fractures as shadows drift down the passage behind her. The hallway becomes both stage and liminal space — a place where presence is unsettled, neither fixed nor gone. In this portrait, becoming is not linear but suspended, a corridor where beauty waits to cross into itself.

title: Angelic Intentions
description: Bathed in spectral blue, the figure appears both ethereal and defiant, her shadow anchoring her against the brutalist wall behind. The paint bleeds into the body like divine interference, blurring the line between sanctity and flesh. This is not innocence, but intention — a portrait where angelic grace becomes an act of will, sharpened by fracture and emergence.

title: Kisses of Ruin
description: The mouth becomes both weapon and wound: lips lacquered in darkness, fractured by crimson eruptions. Seduction here is inseparable from destruction, each kiss carrying the trace of collapse. The portrait stages beauty at its most volatile — desire turning into ruin, emergence flickering through the cracks.

title: The Architecture of Burning Silence
description: The figure stands as if built into the geometry around her, her body seared with molten yellows and deep shadows. She becomes both structure and flame — an edifice of stillness pierced by fire. In this portrait, silence is not absence but architecture itself: a space where beauty endures, burning quietly against the weight of the world.

title: Making Blue a Feeling
description: The portrait dissolves into pure chromatic emotion — shards of cobalt and fragments of glitch turn blue into something lived, not seen. The figure hovers between presence and disintegration, embodying melancholy as material, serenity as fracture. Here, blue is no longer a colour: it is a state of being.

title: Falling Between the Cracks
description: In this work, the portrait resists coherence. The figure appears only to disintegrate, her image caught in a fragile state between formation and collapse. Layers of saturated pigment and digital erosion stage a dialogue between beauty and disappearance, suggesting that identity itself is never fixed but always emergent. What begins as a likeness dissolves into painterly abstraction, a visual record of the machine’s faltering attempt to hold memory in place.

title: Passing the Buck
description: Here, the portrait ignites. The figure is consumed by eruptions of molten yellow and black, as if beauty itself were on fire and refusing to be controlled. Against the cold grid of architecture, she becomes volatile, unstable — an image in revolt. This is emergence not as quiet evolution, but as combustion: the moment identity breaks open, refuses containment, and passes the burden of meaning back onto us.

title: Chromatic Rupture
description: The figure glows through veils of molten colour, her form gently dissolving into waves of orange and blue. What might appear as fracture becomes radiance — an unfolding where beauty is not broken but transformed. Here, the portrait reveals emergence as a quiet rupture, a passage between stillness and becoming.

title: eating the world
description:
Dinner served: 1 planet, medium rare. My little thank-you snack for riding with 1mouth.  
When the mouth opens wide enough, the whole world fits inside.

eating the world, 2025.  
Digital collage.  
2800x3500px

title: Proof of Faith
description:
From the series "Adolescence"

Illustrated by Ballzerino 2025
Format: JPG 5000 x 5000 px

title: MATRIARCHY RULES
description: Glitch Loop Collage with AI Track - 'Glitch Me", Miss AL Simpson, 2025

title: DRONE RED CARPET
description: Drone Red Carpet fuses fashion and surveillance into one unforgettable vision. Drones replace the faces of guests, turning the red carpet into a performance of luxury watched — and owned — by machines. Seductive, cinematic, and unsettling, it reimagines glamour for the age of AI.

title: BUTLER WITH SPIES
description:
Brett the Butler watched everything through the lens of his drone-cam — a device designed to look like a human eye, but capable of recording with Hollywood precision. In L.A., discretion was currency, and Brett had mastered it. He bleached his hair to a sugar-daddy grey, just enough to appear charming without being a threat. The disguise worked every time.

Behind the façade, Brett kept a paparazzi ledger that could ruin half the city. His side hustle — selling out semi-famous starlets and fading icons — was more profitable than service ever was. Soon he would need another name, another skin, another life. But for now, he poured the champagne, charged the drones, and captured every secret worth selling.

title: DRONE DANCERS
description:
They stepped out in unison, a dance troupe for the West Hollywood crew.  But above their heads, the drones replaced halos, humming with the quiet menace of control. These weren’t dancers for the stage; they were avatars, choreographed to perform beauty for the machine’s gaze.
Each movement was catalogued, every gesture owned. The dance routine was machine controlled, a spectacle of surveillance with drones disguised as dancers.

title: HOLLYWOOD DOLLS
description:
They sat in a haze of d-cigarette smoke, idly playing with her dressing drones. The “d-Cigarette” was her own invention — born from post-divorce survival and a razor-sharp instinct for opportunity. By fusing drones with e-cigs, she created California’s latest addiction: glossy black machines that people puffed on as if their lives depended on it.

Now the Hollywood dolls couldn’t live without them. She had made her millions off their cravings — selling glamour wrapped in surveillance. It was like taking candy from babies. Very rich babies.

title: DAISY IN LEATHER
description: “Daisy in Leather, Chanel on the floor.” She traded couture for black leather and an Oxy Drone haze, glamour collapsing into raw intoxication. A performance where fashion, addiction, and surveillance fuse into one.

title: PINK FEATHERS AND DRONE
description:
The beautiful blonde stands in the dusk, pink feathers clinging to her like smoke, lips the colour of neon sins. The pool behind her reflects a world already gone — too fragile, too staged. Above, the drone hangs low, black as a bad omen, recording every secret she never meant to give away.

The paint scrawls her story into the surface: Drones of Suburbia. Glamour as armour. Surveillance as seduction. A moment caught between beauty and annihilation — where even the sky feels complicit.

title: HOLLYWOOD DRONES (THE DRONES OF SUBURBIA)
description:
AI Film Artwork by Miss AL Simpson

In Hollywood Drones, Miss AL Simpson extends her Drones of Suburbia series into the sunset-saturated streets of Los Angeles. Here, the eternal glamour of Hollywood collides with the omniscient gaze of machines. Drone eyes replace paparazzi, red-lit butlers deploy them as silent spies inside mirrored villas, and hackers wage midnight battles to turn their signals against themselves.

The film unfolds as a fever dream: starlets smoking D-cigarettes beneath rose-pink skies, models glowing against vending machines, red carpets shimmering with mechanical angels. But beneath the spectacle lies paranoia—private lives recorded, secrets bought and sold, beauty itself weaponized.

Simpson merges the cinematic language of noir, fashion photography, and sci-fi futurism into a new genre of AI film art. Hollywood Drones is not just a vision of Los Angeles, but a meditation on control, visibility, and the fragile allure of power under algorithmic rule.

title: MISS AL SIMPSON X DALL-E 2 - LOST MOTEL 51
collection: MISS AL SIMPSON x DALL-E 2 - LOST MOTELS
description:
Originally generated using DALL-E 2 in 2022, and downloaded by Miss AL Simpson from labs.openai.com.

The DALL-E 2 model is now deprecated. These images cannot be regenerated or re-created using current models.

Part of the DALL-E MOTELS archive — a curated collection of neural artefacts salvaged from the lost memory of a machine.

This work represents one of the last surviving visual hallucinations from a now-extinct generative AI model.
